title: 10 best herbal creams for headaches

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lavender's antispasmodic properties help to relax muscle tension and relieve spasms that can contribute to headache pain.

Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) properties: Lavender's anxiolytic effects can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for headaches and migraines, allowing the body to respond more effectively to pain relief.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in blood vessels that can contribute to headache pain and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with headaches because of its soothing and calming properties. The active compounds in lavender oil, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have a sedative effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can trigger headaches. Additionally, lavender creams can help relax tense muscles and improve sleep quality, which are common factors contributing to headache development. Regular use of lavender creams may provide relief from recurring headaches and promote overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: It acts as a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce pain and inflammation associated with headaches.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene has a sedative effect, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension, which are common triggers for headaches.
Caryophyllene oxide: It has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation caused by headaches, while also promoting relaxation and reducing stress.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Lavandula angustifolia are used in creams for headaches due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: The flowers are used in creams for headaches due to their analgesic and antispasmodic properties, which help relieve pain and muscle tension.

Essential oil from flowers: Although not directly listed, the most common part used is the essential oil which is extracted from the flowers. The essential oil from flowers is used in creams for headaches due to its ability to ease pain and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 bunches of  lavandula angustifolia flowers on a warm sunny day for a rich fragrance.
Dry the harvested flowers in a low temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Steep 1 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in 2 cups of boiling distilled water for 10 minutes.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax pellets with 1/2 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of shea butter until smooth.
Combine the infused liquid with the wax mixture and whip until thick and creamy in 10 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint creams contain flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, alleviating headache symptoms by constricting or dilating blood vessels as needed.
Pain-relieving properties: The menthol and menthone in peppermint creams have analgesic and anti-nociceptive effects, helping to numb pain receptors and relieve headache discomfort.

Digestive-soothing properties: Peppermint creams can help alleviate digestive issues that may contribute to or exacerbate headaches, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) symptoms, by reducing spasms and improving gut function.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with headaches because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol and menthone present in peppermint oil have a cooling effect on the skin, which can help to relax tense muscles and improve blood flow to the affected area. This can help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with headaches. Additionally, the calming and soothing effect of peppermint can also help to reduce stress and promote relaxation, further alleviating headache symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Acts as a natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce pain and inflammation associated with headaches.
Menthone: Exhibits analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, contributing to its efficacy in relieving headache symptoms, such as pain and pressure.
Caryophyllene: A sesquiterpene with anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which may help alleviate headache symptoms by reducing inflammation and modulating pain pathways.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The primary component used to make creams for headaches, as they contain high concentrations of menthol and menthone, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: Also used in creams for headaches, as they contain menthol and other compounds that help to relax muscles and reduce pain.
Leaves: Additionally, the leaves are used to make creams for headaches, as they are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the mind and body.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers, clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Steep 50g of the harvested plant material in 250ml of jojoba oil in a double boiler at 45 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discard the solids.
Mix 20g of beeswax with 50g of shea butter and 20g of vitamin e oil in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for 10 minutes.
Combine the infused oil with the beeswax mixture, stir until fully incorporated and pour into tin containers to cool and solidify.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as camphor and borneol, help to reduce swelling and pain in the head and neck, alleviating tension headache symptoms.

Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic properties of sage, including thujone, can relax muscle contractions and spasms that contribute to migraines and other types of headaches, providing relief from pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Sage cream's antioxidant compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation, which are often underlying causes of headaches, including tension and migraines.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, creams helps with headaches because of its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. The active compounds in sage, such as camphor and borneol, have a soothing effect on the nervous system, calming tension and reducing pain. Additionally, sage's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to headache development. By applying sage cream topically, individuals can experience relief from headache symptoms, including pain, tension, and fatigue, making it a natural and effective remedy for headaches.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with headaches.
Salvianolic acid: This phenolic acid has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities that help alleviate headache symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.
Bornyl acetate: This terpene has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with headaches, providing relief from headache symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of salvene, which is believed to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Used due to their ability to provide relief from headaches through their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Stems: Used due to their ability to provide a base for the extraction of compounds that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, contributing to headache relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh salvia officinalis leaves on a sunny morning for optimal potency and aroma.
Infuse the leaves in 100ml of cold-pressed olive oil for 2 weeks to extract the active compounds.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a clear extract.
Mix the infused oil with 50g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture constantly and heat it to 180°f for 10 minutes to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemon balm cream helps to relax tense muscles and calm spasms, which can contribute to headache pain, by reducing muscle contractions and promoting relaxation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which is often a contributing factor to headaches such as migraines and tension headaches.

Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: Lemon balm has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate anxiety-related headache triggers and promote relaxation, reducing the frequency and severity of headaches.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, creams helps with headaches because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal extract contains rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to relax tense muscles and reduce pain. Additionally, lemon balm creams have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to alleviate stress and anxiety that can contribute to headaches. By applying the cream topically, individuals can experience relief from tension and migraine headaches, promoting relaxation and overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that helps reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with headaches by acting as a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent.
Linalool: A terpene with analgesic and sedative properties, which helps relieve headache symptoms by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension.
Limonene: A terpene with a calming effect, which helps to reduce anxiety and alleviate headache symptoms by promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in essential oils and flavonoids, providing a calming and soothing effect on headaches.
Stems: Containing bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, contributing to headache relief.
Roots: Acting as a natural anti-inflammatory agent, the roots help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with headaches.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried melissa flowers in a carrier oil like sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:5 for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discard the solids.
Mix the infused oil with a beeswax and shea butter blend at a ratio of 1:0.5:0.25 by weight.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler to melt the beeswax and mix until smooth.
Pour the melted mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify completely.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Maypop cream's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling and pain associated with headaches, providing fast relief from tension headaches, migraines, and other headache types.
Pain-relieving: The cream's analgesic properties, derived from the Passiflora incarnata plant, work to dull or eliminate headache pain, making it an effective natural remedy for headache management.

Sedative and calming effects: Maypop cream's sedative and calming properties can help alleviate stress, anxiety, and insomnia, which are common triggers for headaches, promoting relaxation and reducing headache frequency.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata, also known as maypop, creams helps with headaches because of its natural sedative properties and ability to calm the nervous system. The active compounds in Passiflora incarnata, including flavonoids and alkaloids, work to reduce inflammation and relax tense muscles, which can contribute to headache pain. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress, these creams can help alleviate headache symptoms and provide relief from tension and migraine headaches. This natural approach can also promote overall well-being and reduce reliance on medication.
remedy_constituents: Isorhapontigenin: This phenolic compound has been shown to have a sedative and anti-inflammatory effect, helping to reduce the severity and frequency of headaches.

Flavonoids (e.g., kaempferol, quercetin): These plant-based compounds possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate headache symptoms by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body.
Harman and Harmine Alkaloids: These alkaloids have been found to possess sedative and antispasmodic properties, which can help to relax muscles and reduce the frequency and severity of headaches.
remedy_parts: Roots: Alkaloids present in the roots of Passiflora incarnata are used in creams for headaches due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The flavonoids in the leaves of Passiflora incarnata contribute to their use in creams for headaches, providing a sedative effect.
Fruits: The juice from the fruits of Passiflora incarnata is used in creams for headaches due to its content of flavonoids and alkaloids, which have a calming effect.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves from the plant.
Dry the harvested passiflora incarnata in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Steep 1 cup of dried passiflora incarnata in 2 cups of carrier oil such as coconut oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after 1 hour.
Blend the infused oil with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of shea butter to create a creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal aloe creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which can help reduce inflammation in the body, including in blood vessels and muscles, to alleviate headache symptoms.

Pain-relieving agents: Aloe vera gel contains a variety of pain-relieving compounds, including salicylic acid (similar to aspirin), which can help to block the production of pain-causing chemicals in the brain and provide relief from headaches.

Antioxidants and anti-stress compounds: Herbal aloe creams contain antioxidants like vitamin C and beta-carotene, as well as anti-stress compounds like ashwagandha, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety-related headaches by protecting the body against oxidative stress and promoting relaxation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with headaches because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from the aloe plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which reduce swelling and ease tension in the muscles and blood vessels. This helps to alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with headaches. Additionally, the soothing and cooling effect of aloe vera can help to calm the mind and body, providing relief from tension headaches and migraines. Its natural properties make it an effective remedy for headache relief.
remedy_constituents: Bradykininase: It helps with headaches by breaking down bradykinin, a peptide that causes blood vessels to dilate and leads to pain and inflammation in the head.
Aloin: It helps with headaches by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the release of pain-causing neurotransmitters, thus providing relief from headache pain.
Anthraquinones (specifically, aloemodin): It helps with headaches by acting as a vasodilator, which helps to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow to the head, thereby reducing headache pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Aloe vera are the most commonly used part for making creams for headaches due to their rich content of aloe-emodin, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: Aloe vera seeds contain aloin, a compound that has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them a popular choice for creams aimed at relieving headache pain.
Stems: The stems of Aloe vera are often used to extract aloin, which is then used in creams for headaches due to its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest aloe vera gel by cutting off the top and bottom of the leaves and gently squeezing out the gel.
Weigh 200 grams of aloe vera gel and mix it with 100 grams of distilled water in a large bowl.
Combine 50 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil in a small saucepan and heat it over low heat.
Add the aloe vera gel mixture to the saucepan and stir well to combine the ingredients and heat for 10 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Chamomile creams have antispasmodic properties that help relax tense muscles and calm nerve endings, which can help alleviate headache symptoms such as muscle tension and spasms.
Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in chamomile creams reduce swelling and pain associated with headaches, including migraines and tension headaches.
Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) property: Chamomile's anxiolytic effects can help calm the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety that can trigger or exacerbate headaches.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with headaches because of its natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The apigenin present in chamomile has a calming effect on the mind and body, reducing tension and stress that often lead to headaches. Additionally, its analgesic properties help to ease pain and discomfort associated with headaches, providing quick relief and promoting relaxation. This herbal remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate headaches, making it a popular and effective natural solution.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid acts as a sedative and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce pain and soothe headaches by promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation.
Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate headache symptoms by reducing muscle tension and inflammation in the affected areas.

Matricine: This sesquiterpene lactone has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and sedative properties, which can help calm headache symptoms by relaxing muscles, reducing inflammation, and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain apigenin, an antioxidant that can help calm the nervous system and reduce headache symptoms.
Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that can help alleviate headache pain.
Seeds: They contain matricine, a sesquiterpene lactone that has anti-inflammatory and sedative effects, which can help soothe headaches and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers in the early morning when they are dry.
Dry the harvested matricaria chamomilla flowers in a warm place at 50 degrees celsius for 3 hours.
Steep 20 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in 100 ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused matricaria chamomilla oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container in 10 minutes.
Blend 10 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil into the matricaria chamomilla oil mixture in 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: St. John's Wort creams contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to headache development.
Pain relief: The active compounds in St. John's Wort, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that may help relieve pain associated with headaches.
Anti-inflammatory effects: St. John's Wort creams may reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and surrounding tissues, which can help alleviate headache symptoms such as tension and pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, creams helps with headaches because they contain hypericin and hyperforin, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. These compounds work by inhibiting the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body and reducing inflammation in the blood vessels. By applying these creams topically, they can directly target the affected area, providing fast and effective relief from tension headaches, migraines, and other types of headaches. This natural remedy offers a convenient and non-invasive way to manage headache symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: This prenylated phenolic compound has potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with headaches.

Hypotensive flavonoids (e.g. kaempferol, quercetin): These flavonoids have a vasodilatory effect, which helps to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, reducing the severity and frequency of headaches.
Naphthodianthrones (e.g. hypericin): These compounds have a mild sedative effect, which can help to reduce anxiety and stress that may contribute to headache development and severity.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to relieve headache pain.
Flowers: Used for their flavonoids and phloroglucinol, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects to ease headache symptoms.
Roots: Used for their phloroglucinol content, which has a sedative effect and can help to reduce headache pain and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of fresh hypericum perforatum flowers on a sunny day after the morning dew.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 1-2 weeks.
Create a tincture by steeping 1 cup of dried hypericum perforatum in 2 cups of vodka for 2 weeks.
Mix 1/4 cup of the tincture with 1/2 cup of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a double boiler.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer until it thickens and turns into a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Anxiolytic property: Herbal valerian creams contain compounds like valerenic acid, which have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and stress-related headaches by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension.
Pain-relieving property: Valerian root has been shown to possess analgesic properties, helping to alleviate headache pain by inhibiting the release of pain-causing neurotransmitters in the brain.

Antispasmodic property: Herbal valerian creams contain compounds that can help relax smooth muscle tissue, which may help relieve tension headaches by reducing the spasms and contractions that often contribute to headache pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian, creams helps with headaches because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in valerian, such as valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, work to relax tense muscles, reduce pain, and improve blood flow to the affected areas. By applying valerian cream topically, the soothing effects can be directly delivered to the skin, providing quick relief from tension headaches and migraines, allowing individuals to find comfort and relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Isovaleric acid: Isovaleric acid, a short-chain fatty acid, has been shown to have a sedative effect, which can help alleviate headache symptoms by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension.

Valerenic acid: Valerenic acid, a sesquiterpene, has been found to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce the frequency and severity of headaches by modulating the body's response to pain.
Valerenol: Valerenol, another sesquiterpene, has been shown to have a sedative and antispasmodic effect, which can help relieve headache symptoms by relaxing tense muscles and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Valeriana officinalis are used to make creams for headaches due to their high valerenic acid content, which has a calming effect on the mind and body.
Roots: The roots are also used for their sedative properties, which help to alleviate headache symptoms by promoting relaxation and reducing stress.

Roots: In addition to their sedative effects, the roots of Valeriana officinalis contain valerenal, a compound that has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating headache pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots in the evening after the full moon when the plant is in its prime.
Dry valeriana officinalis roots at 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind 100 grams of dried valeriana officinalis roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 500 grams of distilled water with 100 grams of valeriana officinalis powder in a double boiler.
Add 20 grams of beeswax to the mixture and stir until it reaches a thick creamy consistency.

remedy_properties: Feverfew: Feverfew contains a compound called parthenolide, which has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that can help reduce headache severity.
Peppermint: Peppermint oil's menthol content helps to relax muscles, ease tension, and improve blood flow, which can help alleviate tension headaches.
Lavender: Lavender tea's calming effects on the nervous system can help reduce anxiety and stress, common triggers for migraines and tension headaches.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis, also known as tea, creams helps with headaches because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds found in Camellia sinensis, such as theaflavins and thearubigins, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Topical application of Camellia sinensis creams can provide quick relief from tension headaches by relaxing tense muscles and improving blood circulation. Regular use can also help to reduce the frequency and severity of headaches, providing a natural and effective solution for those suffering from this common condition.
remedy_constituents: Quercetin: A flavonoid phenolic compound that helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, contributing to the relief of headache symptoms.
Epicatechin: A flavan-3-ol compound with antioxidant properties that may help prevent blood vessel constriction and reduce pain associated with headaches.
Theaflavins: A type of catechin that may help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the brain, potentially alleviating headache symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are a popular choice due to their high content of caffeine and theobromine, which are known for their pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: Camellia sinensis buds are used for their potential analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate headache symptoms.
Stems: The stems of Camellia sinensis contain compounds that may help reduce pain and inflammation, making them a viable option for use in headache creams.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 250g of camellia sinensis leaves in 1000ml of boiling distilled water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean container to separate leaves from liquid.
Combine 500g of distilled water with 100g of glycerin and 50g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Add the camellia sinensis infusion to the mixture in the double boiler and heat until beeswax melts.
Remove the double boiler from heat and let the mixture cool and thicken for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with headaches because they contain natural compounds that relax the muscles, ease tension, and soothe the nerves. The anise-like aroma of fennel has a calming effect on the mind and body, reducing stress and promoting relaxation. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling in the blood vessels, which can contribute to headache pain. As a result, applying herbal fennel creams to the neck, forehead, or scalp can provide quick relief from tension headaches, migraines, and other types of headache pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to headache development and severity.
Pain-relieving properties: Fennel contains a compound called anethole, which has analgesic (pain-reducing) and sedative effects, helping to alleviate headache pain and discomfort.

Antispasmodic properties: Fennel's antispasmodic compounds can help relax muscle tension, reduce spasm frequency and severity, and alleviate migraines and other types of headaches characterized by spasms or contractions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving headache pain.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain a compound called anethole, which has natural sedative properties, helping to calm tension headaches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare dried leaves and stems, weighing 100 grams for each 250 milliliters of cream base preparation.
Combine the dried foeniculum vulgare herb with 250 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place allowing the mixture to steep for at least 4 hours and up to 8 hours, shaking occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and preserving the infused oil.
Mix the infused oil with beeswax, weighing 20 grams per 250 milliliters of oil, melting the mixture in a double boiler for about 10 minutes preparation time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange creams helps with headaches because they contain a unique blend of herbal extracts that work synergistically to alleviate pain. The bitter orange peel, in particular, is rich in citrus flavonoids that have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce blood vessel dilation and constrict excessive blood flow that can cause migraine pain. Additionally, the creams' soothing and warming properties help to relax tense muscles and ease tension headaches, providing rapid relief from headache discomfort.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: These antioxidants have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which can help reduce headache severity by reducing inflammation and relaxing blood vessels.
Vitamin C: High levels of vitamin C in bitter orange creams can help constrict blood vessels, reducing inflammation and relieving headache symptoms such as throbbing or pulsating sensations.

Essential Oils: The essential oils present in bitter orange creams, such as citrus peel oil, have natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help ease headache pain and reduce tension.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in bioactive compounds, rhyzomes have been traditionally used to alleviate headache symptoms by providing anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Barks: The barks of the bitter orange plant contain flavonoids, which help to relax blood vessels, reducing tension headaches and migraine pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried citrus x aurantium peel and grind it into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground citrus peel powder with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil and 10 drops of lavender essential oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 24 hours at room temperature, allowing the citrus peel to infuse into the oil and release its active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Add 20 grams of beeswax and heat the mixture in a double boiler until the wax melts, then pour the cream into sterilized tubes or containers for use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with headaches because they contain a unique compound called glycyrrhizin, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically to the skin, glycyrrhizin can help reduce swelling and ease tension in the head and neck, providing relief from migraines, tension headaches, and other types of pain. Additionally, licorice creams often contain soothing ingredients like aloe vera and camphor that can calm irritated nerves and promote relaxation, further reducing headache discomfort.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: A compound found in licorice root, glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which can help to reduce headache severity.

Flavonoids: The flavonoid content in herbal licorice creams can help to improve blood flow to the brain, reducing migraine and tension headache symptoms by alleviating vasoconstriction and promoting vasodilation.

Glycerrhetic acid: A metabolite of glycyrrhizin, glycerrhetic acid has a similar pain-relieving effect as ibuprofen and aspirin, but with fewer side effects, making it an effective natural treatment for headache relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with headaches.
Barks: Contain triterpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing headache-related pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots weighing approximately 500 grams for infusion.
Combine the harvested glycyrrhiza glabra roots with 1000 milliliters of distilled water in a large saucepan and simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid extract.
Melt 500 grams of beeswax and combine it with the reserved liquid extract in a heat-proof mixing bowl and stir until well combined.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify at room temperature before transferring it to an airtight container for storage or use as desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with headaches because they contain anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can effectively soothe and calm the mind and body. The flavonoids present in dandelions have been shown to reduce inflammation and ease pain, making them a natural remedy for headache relief. Additionally, the creams' cooling and soothing effects can help relax tense muscles and improve blood circulation, which can also contribute to alleviating headaches.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion cream contains sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce pain and swelling associated with headaches.

Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in dandelion cream, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which is thought to contribute to the development of headaches.
Pain-relieving: Dandelion contains a compound called taraxasterol, which has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making it effective in reducing headache pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it an effective ingredient for relieving headache pain.
Buds: Containing a high concentration of flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the brain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh dandelion flowers and leaves from a pesticide-free area for infusions.
Combine the harvested herbs with 500ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container for 10 minutes.
Steep the mixture for 20 minutes, then strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container.
Mix 200g of beeswax and 100g of coconut oil together, then slowly add the strained herb infusion while stirring until emulsified.
Pour the herbal cream mixture into clean glass jars, label, and store in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose creams helps with headaches because of its potent anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as beta-carotene and lycopene, work together to reduce pain and inflammation in the scalp, providing quick relief from tension headaches and migraines. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to relax tense muscles and improve blood circulation, further contributing to its headache-relieving effects.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic properties: These properties help to reduce inflammation and relax tense muscles, which can contribute to the development of migraines and tension headaches.

Antioxidant properties: Rose extracts are rich in antioxidants, which help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the body, which may exacerbate headache symptoms such as pain and sensitivity to light and sound.

Sedative and calming properties: The essential oils present in damask rose creams have a sedative effect, helping to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation, which can be beneficial for individuals who experience frequent or severe headaches.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help relieve headache pain.
Seeds: The seeds contain essential oils that can provide a calming effect, helping to reduce tension and alleviate headache symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa damascena flowers at dawn and dry them properly overnight in a well-ventilated area.
Combine dried flowers with sweet almond oil and beeswax in a double boiler, heating slowly over low heat.
Add 10g of dried rose petals per 100ml of oil mixture, infusing for 30 minutes under gentle heat.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding solids and preserving the liquid.
Allow the herbal infusion to cool and thicken before pouring it into airtight containers for storage at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Black pepper creams helps with headaches because they contain piperine, a bioactive compound that has natural anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, piperine interacts with the body's pain receptors, reducing inflammation and constricting blood vessels that may be contributing to the headache. Additionally, piperine also blocks the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that can cause blood vessel dilation and further exacerbate headache symptoms. By addressing these underlying causes, herbal black pepper creams can provide effective relief from tension headaches and migraines.
remedy_properties: Piperine: Piperine, a bioactive compound found in black pepper, has potent anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making it effective in reducing headache symptoms.
Vasodilation: The cream's vasodilatory effects help to dilate blood vessels, which can reduce tension headaches caused by constricted blood flow and improve overall circulation to the brain.

Analgesic and anti-inflammatory action: The combination of black pepper and other herbs in the cream provides analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate headache pain, inflammation, and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: These underground stems have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving headache pain.
Barks: The bark contains piperine, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, which can help alleviate headache symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 200g of dried piper nigrum, 100ml of sweet almond oil, and 50g of beeswax.
Grind the dried piper nigrum into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the ground piper nigrum with 100ml of sweet almond oil in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Melt the 50g of beeswax in the double boiler with the piper nigrum mixture for an additional 20 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify at room temperature for 2 hours before transferring to an airtight container.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for headaches are topical creams infused with natural herbs and essential oils that provide relief from migraine and tension headaches. They help by reducing pain, inflammation, and promoting relaxation. The best herbal creams for headaches include Foeniculum vulgare, Lavandula angustifolia, Mentha x piperita, Melissa officinalis, Zingiber officinale, Valeriana officinalis, Ginkgo biloba, Achillea millefolium, Salvia officinalis, and Cinnamomum verum. If left untreated, headaches can significantly impact daily life, leading to missed work, strained relationships, and decreased productivity, ultimately affecting overall well-being and quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to headaches that are treated with herbal creams are muscle and joint pain, arthritis, and minor burns. Herbal creams containing ingredients such as arnica, calendula, and peppermint can help reduce inflammation, ease discomfort, and promote healing. These natural remedies can provide relief from chronic conditions like fibromyalgia and osteoarthritis, as well as acute injuries like sprains and strains.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with headaches is a blend of peppermint, eucalyptus, and lavender. Peppermint oil has a calming effect on the mind and body, while eucalyptus oil helps to reduce inflammation and relieve tension. Lavender oil, with its soothing properties, promotes relaxation and reduces stress. When combined, these herbal creams work together to ease headache symptoms, provide relief from pain, and promote overall well-being. Regular application can help alleviate frequent headaches and promote a healthier, happier you.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Cephalalgia : an international journal of headache, medicinal plant-based creams can be a good alternative for treating headaches due to their pharmacologically active properties. The study, conducted in Nepal, found that out of 46 individuals with probable medication-overuse headache, 13% (6/46) were using medicinal plants, suggesting their potential role in headache causation. The use of medicinal plant-based creams may help alleviate headache symptoms without contributing to medication-overuse headache, making them a safer option for long-term treatment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare creams helps with headaches because of its potent anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, derived from the herb's essential oils. The aniseed-like fragrance of fennel oil, found in Foeniculum vulgare, has been traditionally used to ease tension and relax muscles, which can contribute to headaches. Additionally, the herb's ability to reduce stress and promote relaxation can help alleviate migraine symptoms and other types of headaches, providing natural relief and comfort to those affected. Its calming effects can bring quick relief.
remedy_constituents: Foeniculin: It has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation associated with headaches.
Anethole: It has a sedative effect, which can help to relax tense muscles and reduce stress that may contribute to headaches.
Phenolic acids: They have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that may exacerbate headache symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Foeniculum vulgare leaves are commonly used to create creams due to their high content of volatile oils, particularly anethole, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: Foeniculum vulgare seeds are used in creams because they contain a high concentration of anethole, a natural compound that helps alleviate headaches and other pain-related issues.

Roots: Foeniculum vulgare roots are utilized in creams for their bioactive compounds, including coumarins and furocoumarins, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in treating headaches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of foeniculum vulgare flowers when they are in full bloom for optimal potency.
Dry the flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours or until crispy.
Combine 1 cup of dried flowers with 1 cup of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Simmer the mixture at 160°f for 2 hours to infuse the oil with the herb's properties.
Strain the mixture and whip it with 2 tablespoons of beeswax to create a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with headaches because the calming and soothing properties of lavender oil can ease tension and stress that often contribute to headaches. The aroma of lavender oil can promote relaxation, reduce anxiety, and promote better sleep, all of which can help alleviate headache symptoms. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of lavender oil may also help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with headaches, providing quick and natural relief. This makes Lavandula angustifolia creams a popular and effective remedy for headache sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate headache pain and reduce inflammation associated with headaches.
Linalyl acetate: This ester has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing muscle tension and promoting relaxation, which can help alleviate headache symptoms.
Caffeic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that may contribute to headache development.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Flowers: They are used for their calming and relaxing effects, which help to reduce headache severity and promote better sleep.
Stems: They are used due to their rich content of flavonoids and terpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers when they reach full bloom and are in optimal color.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm place for 7 to 10 days to remove excess moisture completely.
Infuse 2 cups of dried flowers in 1 quart of carrier oil like sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a fragrant oil.
Mix the infused oil with 1 cup of beeswax and 1 cup of shea butter in a double boiler.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams helps with headaches because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. Peppermint oil, the active ingredient, relaxes tense muscles and improves blood flow to the brain, which can help alleviate tension headaches. The menthol content also cools the skin, providing a soothing effect that can ease pain and discomfort. Additionally, peppermint oil has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that often contribute to headache development.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol helps with headaches by acting as a natural pain reliever and cooling agent, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation in the head and neck region.
Limonene: Limonene helps with headaches by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the blood vessels and tissues of the head and neck.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid helps with headaches by acting as a natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, which can help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the blood vessels and tissues of the head and neck.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Mentha x piperita in creams for headaches due to their high concentration of menthol, which provides a cooling effect and helps relieve pain.
Stems: Stems are also used in creams for headaches as they contain menthol and other compounds that help to relax muscles and reduce pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh mentha x piperita leaves from a clean garden patch.
Steam distill the harvested leaves to extract essential oils for 2 hours.
Combine 1/4 cup of the extracted essential oils with 1 cup of distilled water.
Mix 1 cup of beeswax with 1/2 cup of shea butter in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Blend the essential oil mixture with the cooled beeswax mixture to create a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis creams helps with headaches because of its soothing and relaxing properties. The active compounds in Melissa, such as linalool and rosmarinic acid, have a calming effect on the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety that can trigger headaches. Additionally, Melissa officinalis has natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with headaches. This natural remedy can provide fast relief and promote overall well-being. Regular use may also help to reduce the frequency and severity of headaches.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic acid compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce pain and inflammation associated with headaches.
Linalool: A terpene compound, linalool has a calming and sedative effect, which can help alleviate tension headaches and promote relaxation.
Limonene: A terpene compound, limonene has a sedative and antispasmodic effect, which can help reduce muscle spasms and alleviate migraine headaches.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are a primary source due to their high content of melissic acid, which is believed to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: The stems are used for their potential to contribute to the overall medicinal properties of the Melissa officinalis extracts, enhancing the effects of the leaves.
Flowers: The flowers are a rich source of essential oils, which contain compounds that can help to relax and soothe the mind and body, relieving headache symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Infuse melissa officinalis leaves in 1 liter of boiling distilled water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl to remove herb particles.
Mix 100 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Combine 200 milliliters of the infused melissa officinalis liquid with 200 grams of coconut oil.
Blend the mixture of beeswax, shea butter, and coconut oil until it reaches a smooth consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with headaches because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The active compound, gingerol, found in ginger, targets the pain pathways in the brain, reducing the severity of headaches. Additionally, ginger has natural vasodilatory effects, which help to relax blood vessels and improve blood circulation to the brain. This combination of mechanisms makes ginger creams an effective natural remedy for relieving headaches and promoting overall well-being. Regular use may also help in preventing future episodes.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which help to reduce the severity of headaches by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols possess anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which help to alleviate headache symptoms by inhibiting the activity of pain receptors.
Zingerone: This compound has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, contributing to the relief of headache symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Zingiber officinale contain compounds like gingerols and shogaols that have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Roots: The roots of the plant are also a rich source of gingerols and shogaols, making them useful for relieving headache pain.
Buds: The buds of Zingiber officinale contain volatile oils, including gingerol, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate headache symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes from well-drained soil under full sun for 3-4 months.
Dry the harvested rhizomes in a low-temperature oven at 30 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind 10 grams of dried zingiber officinale into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Infuse 50 grams of distilled water in a saucepan with the ground zingiber officinale powder for 15 minutes.
Mix 5 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil in a saucepan and heat until melted for 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis creams helps with headaches because they contain the active compound valerenic acid, which has potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The soothing and calming effects of valerian root reduce muscle tension and relax the mind, providing relief from tension headaches and migraines. Additionally, valerian's natural sedative properties can help regulate sleep patterns, which is often disrupted by frequent headaches, ultimately leading to a reduction in headache frequency and severity. This natural remedy can offer a safe and effective solution for those seeking relief from headaches.
remedy_constituents: Isovaleric acid: This compound helps with headaches by acting as a natural sedative and analgesic, reducing pain and inflammation in the body.
Valerenic acid: Valerenic acid has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate headache symptoms by reducing anxiety and stress.
Valeranone: Valeranone has a calming effect on the body, which can help relax tense muscles and reduce headache-causing stress and anxiety.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing valerenic acid, a key compound that helps in reducing anxiety and stress, contributing to relief from headaches.
Leaves: Valerian leaf extracts have anti-inflammatory properties that help in soothing headache symptoms and reducing pain.
Flowers: Valerian flowers contain valerenic acid and other compounds that contribute to their sedative and calming effects, helping in alleviating headache pain.
remedy_recipe: Combine 10 grams of dried valeriana officinalis root and 100 grams of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler for 2 hours at 60-70 degrees celsius.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Add 5 grams of beeswax and 5 grams of emulsifying wax to the liquid mixture and heat until melted.
Pour the mixture into glass jars and allow to cool and solidify completely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with headaches because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredients, particularly flavonoids and terpenoids, work to improve blood circulation to the brain, reducing tension and pressure that often lead to headaches. By promoting healthy blood flow, Ginkgo biloba creams also help to relax blood vessels and ease pain. This natural remedy provides relief from both migraines and tension headaches, offering a soothing and effective solution for those seeking a non-pharmacological approach to headache management.
remedy_constituents: Ginkgolides: These labdane diterpene trilactones have anti-inflammatory and antiplatelet properties, which can help alleviate migraine headaches by reducing blood vessel constriction and inflammation in the brain.
Bilobalide: This sesquiterpene trilactone has neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help prevent migraine headaches by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain.

Flavonoids (Quercetin and Kaempferol): These polyphenolic compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate migraine headaches by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels and the brain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate headache symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds are used for their unique combination of flavonoids and bilobalide, which have been shown to improve blood flow to the brain and reduce headache frequency and severity.
Barks: The barks are used for their ginkgolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce pain and swelling associated with headaches.
remedy_recipe: Measure and combine 20 grams of ginkgo biloba powder with 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes while occasionally stirring until the beeswax is fully melted.
Add 10 milliliters of jojoba oil and 10 milliliters of sweet almond oil to the mixture and stir well.
Continue heating the mixture for another 5 minutes or until it reaches a temperature of 120 degrees fahrenheit.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool for 30 minutes before transferring it to an airtight container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium creams helps with headaches because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. The herb, also known as yarrow, has been traditionally used to soothe and calm the nervous system, which can be triggered by headaches. The cream's active ingredients, such as sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, work to reduce swelling and ease tension in the muscles and blood vessels, providing relief from headache symptoms and promoting relaxation. This natural remedy can offer a gentle and non-invasive approach to managing headaches.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation associated with headaches.
Nepetalactone: A terpene that has sedative and antispasmodic effects, helping to relax muscles and calm the nervous system, which can contribute to headache relief.
Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic acid that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that may contribute to headache development.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high content of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate headache symptoms.
Roots: The roots are used for their sedative and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm and soothe the body and mind.
remedy_recipe: Harvest a sufficient quantity of fresh achillea millefolium leaves and flowers approximately 20 minutes after sunrise.
Combine 2 tablespoons of dried achillea millefolium flowers with 1 tablespoon of dried flowers of a complementary herb in a bowl.
Infuse 2 cups of boiling distilled water over 1 cup of the dried herb mixture for 10-15 minutes in a glass container.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth into a stainless steel bowl and discard the solids after 30 minutes.
Combine the infused liquid with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/2 cup of sweet almond oil in a double boiler and heat until the mixture is well combined and has a smooth consistency after 20-25 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis creams helps with headaches because they contain salvinorin A, a potent compound that has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. This herb has been used for centuries to treat a variety of ailments, including headaches. The creams work by reducing inflammation and pain in the affected area, providing relief from tension and migraine headaches. Additionally, Salvia officinalis creams may also help to relax the muscles and improve blood flow, further reducing the severity and frequency of headaches.
remedy_constituents: Terpenes: They help with headaches by reducing inflammation and relaxing muscles, thereby alleviating pain and discomfort.
Rosmarinic acid: It helps with headaches by acting as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, thereby preventing oxidative stress and inflammation that can lead to headaches.
Carnosic acid: It helps with headaches by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can reduce the severity and frequency of headaches.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Salvia officinalis leaves are used due to their high concentration of salvianolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Stems: Salvia officinalis stems are used because they contain a high amount of salvianol, a compound with analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.

Roots: Salvia officinalis roots are used due to their richness in iridoid glycosides, which have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate headache symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Extract 250 grams of fresh salvia officinalis leaves and rinse them with cold water for 3 minutes.
Combine the extracted leaves with 500 milliliters of carrier oil in a glass jar and let it steep at room temperature for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another container and discard the solids.
Add 50 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of shea butter to the liquid mixture and heat it gently in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the double boiler and let it cool and thicken before pouring it into a glass jar for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum creams helps with headaches because of its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The oils and compounds present in Cinnamomum verum, such as cinnamaldehyde, have been shown to relax tense muscles and reduce pain. The cream's topical application allows for quick absorption, providing fast relief from tension and migraine headaches. Additionally, the warming sensation caused by Cinnamomum verum can help dilate blood vessels, increasing blood flow and further alleviating pain. This natural remedy can be an effective alternative to traditional pain management methods.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: Acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing pain and inflammation in the body that contributes to headaches.
Cinnamic acid: Exerts a neuroprotective effect, which helps to mitigate the effects of oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, thereby reducing headache severity.
Linalool: Exhibits a sedative effect, helping to relax tense muscles and calm the nervous system, thereby reducing headache frequency and severity.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Cinnamon leaves are used to make creams for headaches due to their ability to ease pain and reduce inflammation.
Stems: Cinnamon stems are sometimes used to make creams for headaches due to their potential to provide relief from pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried cinnamomum verum bark and sift it to remove any debris and lumps.
Combine the sifted cinnamomum verum with 100g of coconut oil and 20g of beeswax in a heat-proof bowl.
Place the bowl over a double boiler and heat the mixture until the beeswax melts completely in 10-15 minutes.
Remove the bowl from the heat and stir in 10g of vitamin e oil and 5g of shea butter.
Pour the mixture into glass jars and let it cool and solidify for at least 30 minutes.
